item.title: This is Trouble…She might not be as shiny as the other Porsche...
item.description:
View on Instagram


This is Trouble…She might not be as shiny as the other Porsches you see on Instagram these days but I am making it my mission to turn her back to her former glory!I will be restoring her with the help of friends & family. This Instagram page will be dedicated to her journey! Follow along if you’re interested in watching her transformation. #porsche #porsche356 #porscheclassic #porscheclub #porschelife #classiccars #classiccarrestoration #restorationproject #relatable #carguyproblems #carguysbelike #356restoration #thestoryoftrouble #followmyjourney #carmotivation

item.title: We knew she wasn’t perfect but we weren’t quite anticipating...
item.description:
View on Instagram


We knew she wasn’t perfect but we weren’t quite anticipating this much iron oxide… Hence, she was named Trouble. She might have a few flaws but what this does mean is that she’s incredibly original & we’ve made quite a few fun discoveries about her & her past. We’re excited to share these all with you. Please follow along if you’re interested in her journey as we work to restore her to her former glory! Comment below if you think Trouble is a suitable name for her. 🙌🏼#porsche #porsche356 #356 #356b #super90 #porscherestoration #carrestoration #vintagerestoration #vintagecar #classiccar #classicporsche #problemchild #rustbucket #barnfind

item.title: Trouble’s pedal box was barely holding on. A few more years in...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Trouble’s pedal box was barely holding on. A few more years in her leaky barn and it would have probably joined her battery and fallen out the floor to be lost forever.Luckily it didn’t…These pedal boxes are currently priced around £400 online (un restored) so I’m pleased it held on, even if it is super duper rusty.I can work with rust but it’s hard to work with something that doesn’t exist.With some careful taps with a hammer, some wd-40, a few screwdriver slips, a hot air gun and some patience, I managed to take the pedal box to pieces and started cleaning the individual bits up.It never fails to amaze me how well steel can clean up. With some fresh paint and new plating it is starting to look like new again!Would you restore a rusty part like this or just buy a new one?#porsche #porscherestoration #oldporsche #classicporsche #classiccar #classiccarrestoration #cartransformation #carproject #porsche356 #porsche911 #driveclassic #restorationprocess #barnfind #pedalbox #pedalboxrestoration #lovecars

item.title: It’s sometimes feels like you’re hitting your head against a...
item.description:
View on Instagram


It’s sometimes feels like you’re hitting your head against a brick wall when you’re working on (classic) cars…Removing the pitman steering arm from Trouble’s steering box was one of those moments! There isn’t much I need to do to restore Trouble’s steering box. It is made up of a handful of components and if the worm & peg (the two main components in the box) are in good condition, it should be pretty straight forward. All I’ll really need to do is clean it, give it some new paint, install some new seals and rebuild it.I started stripping it down. Following the manual:Step 1: Done.Step 2: Done.Step 3: “Unlock and unscrew retaining nut for drop arm. Pull off drop arm with special tool P 72.”Basically, just undo the clamping nut and pull it off….Easy right? It should have been except, the steering arm wouldn’t just pull off. It was stuck on a taped spline and locked on TIGHT!It seemed that no about of persuasion was going to make it move.The worst bit was that this arm HAD to come off for me to open the up the box and properly assess the condition of the worm and peg.I’ve spent the last two weekends trying to get it off. Then finally on Saturday…BANG.It gave up its grip.Amongst this set back of “lost time” there are some positives…1. I now know tapered splines (especially steering arms) are a bin to remove.2. I spent the last two weekends problem solving with my dad. 3. The moment of joy when it gave will be a memory to last a life time.A serious case of Type 2 fun!What are your worst & best garage moments, share them in the comments!#porscherestoration #classiccarrestoration #carproject #restoration #garagefun #oldcar #oldporsche #porsche #relatable #driveclassic #classiccars #barnfind #shareyourstory

item.title: “Pardon?”“Sorry, say that again?”“Hang on a minute, I ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


“Pardon?”“Sorry, say that again?”“Hang on a minute, I can’t hear.”That’s me. That’s my life now.Years of lawn mowers munching through grass, leaf blowers roaring like jet engines, hammers clanging off the vice, angle grinders spitting sparks, compressors kicking in with an ear-splitting roar and standing too close to racing cars and guess what?I didn’t wear ear defenders.I now have conductive hearing loss AND pulsatile tinnitus as an extra bonus…That means not only am I losing my hearing but I also get a built-in, never-ending background track of whooshing. Neat…not.It’s ironic losing your hearing but at the same time never being able to hear silence again.Anyway, this isn’t a sympathy vote; it’s me sharing my experience. I took my ears for granted and abused them.PSA: Ear defenders are cool. I wish I had worn them earlier. If you enjoy watch practical stuff and classic cars, follow along to see me finally get Trouble on the road. Just don’t be insulted if I don’t hear you cheering….#porscherestoration #classiccarrestoration #carrestoration #carproject #classiccar #builtnotbought #projectcar #iwillfixyou #driveclassic #classiccarinspiration #carinspiration #classiccarsculture #garagereality #relatable #drivevintage #porsche

item.title: I hope my mum, dad and sister (in fact, all relatives) watch thi...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I hope my mum, dad and sister (in fact, all relatives) watch this until the end…You have probably guessed that the only girl in my life is my car.I spend most my spare time in the garage working to get her back on the road and will be doing the same this Friday. However, you’re spending Valentine’s Day, whether it’s with a loved one or the same as me, on your back on the garage floor……have a good one!Follow to find out if Trouble ever gets a passenger!#porsche #porsche356 #porsche911 #classicporsche #oldporsche #valentinesporsche #valentinesreel #classiccarsofinstagram #drivevintage #garagetime #hobby #oldcars #oldschoolcar #rustycar #relatable #sorrymum

item.title: It doesn’t matter who you are, life is hard... We all have our...
item.description:
View on Instagram


It doesn’t matter who you are, life is hard... We all have our own challenges in some shape or form, big or small.I have my own fair share.BUTI’d argue restoring a car actually makes life easier….Trouble is called Trouble for obvious reasons but ironically, she has actually really helped me with my own “troubles”. Any time life isn’t quite going to plan? I go out into the garage. There it’s just me, the tools and whatever I’m working on (and sometimes my camera) and life suddenly gets a whole lot easier. I find any time my mind is preoccupied, problem solving, fabricating and being creative, I’m in a state of “play”. It’s fun. I don’t have time to think about problems, I’m too busy making progress. Yes, a lot doesn’t quite go as the workshop manual specifies BUT the satisfaction and sense of achievement I get when it all eventually goes right and I can sit back and look at my transformation, that’s priceless….it makes any “hard work” worth it. Does going into the garage always help? There are times when you can’t escape your head but for those moments there’s usually some form of car event or a person I need to speak with to help me with something I’m working on…. One way or another, Trouble helps me to get out of my head, meet others & talk with like minded individuals! Who agrees restoring a car actually makes life easier and what project would you take on? Drop it in the comments! If you get what I mean, hit follow for more. #porsche #oldporsche #vintageporsche #porscherestoration #classiccarrestoration #carrestorations #restorationtherapy #mentalhealthmatters #mentalhealth #mensmentalhealth #projectcar #builtnotbought #driveclassics #classiccars #classiccarsdaily #classiccaruk #classiccarsofinstagram

item.title: I really don’t think Trouble would ever get back on the road e...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I really don’t think Trouble would ever get back on the road even if I did have “cocky confidence” and acted like I knew everything…. She was too far gone for quick fixes. It’s not knowing what I’m doing but having the willingness to learn that is going to make Trouble such a special car. I’ve left my ego at the door, swallowed my pride and don’t make out to know anything I don’t know. I’m not “leaving a stone unturned” and to make sure every “stone” I touch is perfect, I’m reaching out to the experts to advise and guide me. I knew I wasn’t ever going to be rebuilding Trouble’s body myself (this was a job for an expert) but I did know I was going to have to do some welding at some point and some pretty structural welding when I mounted Trouble onto a rotisserie. I decided to reach out to some experts to help me…. These experts were my local college who offered a City and Guild night school course in MIG welding. I’m very excited to say that after 14 weeks of a lot of practice I have passed my level 2 in MIG welding. I didn’t think I would EVER have done this if you had asked me this time last year what I would be getting up to. You might not need a certificate in welding to weld but it’s a piece of paper that has given me the confidence to mount Trouble to a rotisserie and suspend her upside down. I’ll let you put your bets in to see if she falls off….. That’ll probably make a great YouTube episode so don’t forget to subscribe if you want to find out! #carrestoration #porscherestoration #welding #mig #migwelding #classiccar #classiccarproject #carproject #driveclassic #saveclassics #classicsofinstagram #carsofinstagram #barnfind #projectcar #learningonthejob #drivenbydreams #vintagecool #oldcar

item.title: I’m not a professional mechanic but from my experience of gett...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I’m not a professional mechanic but from my experience of getting stuck in, I’ve decided two things:Firstly, I think the rustier the car you can buy for a restoration the better. It really makes the transformations that much more satisfying and worth while.Saying that, there wasn’t actually a whole lot wrong with this little mechanism, other than its appearance. It all worked but was just a bit “crusty”. Whether I should have painted it or not is up for debate (let me know in the comments), I think it made an awesome transformation and leaving it back in bare metal would have been bonkers…just because that’s how it was doesn’t mean it was right…right? Secondly, as controversial as this might be, I don’t believe you need to be a professional mechanic to take on a project like this. Of course it might help but I think the most important thing you need to be is motivated with the right mind set. The rest you can learn. Who agrees?That’s one more piece restored leaving me with about a “million more”….#porscherestoration #porscheproject #classiccar #classiccaruk #classiccarrestoration #restoration #cartransformation #projectcar #barnfind #builtnotbought #drivevintage #oldschool #vintage #fixit #porsche #oldporsche #restorationproject #carsofinstagram

item.title: Who am I kidding!? It’s more like Chapter 200…It’s been ex...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Who am I kidding!? It’s more like Chapter 200…It’s been exactly 480 days since I turned that first screw and started stripping Trouble down to a bare shell.I’ve covered some SERIOUS miles (pun 100% intended) in those days:- Trouble is stripped. - I’ve rebuilt her engine. - I’ve restored her bonnet latch & hinges.- Ive restored her boot latch & hinges.- I’ve restored her pedal box. The list goes on and every “item” I am proud of.BUT The biggest achievement to date is what’s now sat in the garage. Trouble’s 100% rust free body. Getting Trouble’s body back on the straight and narrow was arguably the biggest hurdle of this project and we’ve just cleared it. It’s no secret, I didn’t do the body work. I couldn’t…the reasons why are for another post. But it’s time for me to paint her! 480 days later and we’re starting chapter 2 (or 200): The paint brush….What colour should I paint her? Let me know in the comments. #porscherestoration #carrestoration #classiccarrestoration #classiccar #classiccaruk #porsche #porsche356 #classicporsche #oldporsche #oldcar #rustycar #cartransformation #progress #carsofinstagram #caroftheday #driven #driveclassic #drivenbydreams #vintagecool #barnfind #builtnotbought #bareshell #baremetal

item.title: Just surface rust?! I’m lucky there’s a surface to weld new ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Just surface rust?! I’m lucky there’s a surface to weld new metal too!This was the reality of Trouble’s bodywork and it only got worse once she’d been grit blast…it’s amazing how what appears to be a sold panel can turn into a lace pancake after a session with the grit.I’ve received a few comments along the lines of:- “it’s just surface rust, get her running and drive her as she is” - “keep the patina and just fix the mechanicals”Part of me wishes I could have kept Trouble’s bodywork as it was. It would have been fun driving a car that didn’t look like it should be able to drive! She would have been a true “survivor”, FULL of character with her doors that didn’t shut properly. BUTThat was exactly the problem…her doors didn’t even shut properly! Worse, her pedal box was “floating”…the floor pan it was mounted to was so rusty if you put your foot down on the clutch it would go through the floor!Something had to be done to make her bodywork “work” again but her rust was so wide spread, the ONLY way to see the FULL picture and see just how far the rust went, was to take her back to bare metal.This process sadly removed all of the patina carefully created over the years.Now you can get a better picture of the “full picture”, how many panels do you think I had to replace? Let me know in the comments. #rustyporsche #oldporsche #porsche #porscherestoration #porsche356 #porsche911 #outlaw #carrestoration #carproject #builtnotbought #survivor #rusty #barnfind #justdrive #driveclassic #vintagestyle #classicstyle #caroftheday #carsofinstagram #cartransformation

item.title: Last week I read a news article. I don’t read many news articl...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Last week I read a news article. I don’t read many news articles but this headline caught my eye…“Young adults who cannot tell a spanner from a screwdriver may spell the end of a country of DIY lovers, research suggests.”Halfords (a UK provider of motoring products and services) surveyed 2,012 Gen Z adults (adults aged 18-27 today) and their findings are SHOCKING….Of the Gen Z adults surveyed:- 21% couldn’t identify a spanner - 30% could not identify a flat head screw driver- 50% didn’t know how to add air to a car tyre- Less than half could identify a car jackHalf couldn’t add air to a tyre! Seriously!? Did these kids ride bikes? What did they spend their “youth” doing? Riding around with flat tyres?This research shows it is true that 21% of Gen Z don’t know what a spanner is and 30% don’t know what a flat head screwdriver is. I don’t know the percentage of them that don’t know what a socket is but I haven’t got high hopes…This isn’t a “these kids are stupid” post. This is a “practical skills are clearly being lost” post. Not knowing the difference between a spanner and a screwdriver is terrifying. Not for me, but for our future generations. Lots of people say it doesn’t matter that practical skills are being lost because tech will replace those skills.Who’s going to have the skills to build that tech? We live in a physical world. Computers can’t build bridges, buildings or boats. Even the machines that make our cars these days have a human element in their construction. Without practical skills who is going to create, repair or innovate the technology of tomorrow?Who else finds this research nuts?Regardless of whether you’re Gen Z or not, if you would like to learn more about cars, tools and everything else in between to improve your practical skills and knowledge, follow my journey restoring Trouble. By the end of it, I can guarantee you’ll know what a spanner looks like!I’ll even teach you how to clean a car…the research showed over a quarter didn’t know how do that either!Let’s keep these skills alive for the future!#whatisaspanner Do you think this survey is true or false?
